about:
Virginia public schools are governed by local School Boards under Virginia Department of Education (VDOE) oversight. Virginia law historically required most public schools to begin no earlier than Labor Day — a provision sometimes called the Kings Dominion Law, named partly for the amusement park near Richmond that lobbied to protect summer tourism. That law has since been modified, and many large Virginia districts now begin in late August after obtaining flexibility waivers.

Fairfax County Public Schools is one of the largest school districts in the United States and serves an exceptionally diverse population including federal government employees, military contractors, and diplomatic families from around the world. Prince William County, Loudoun County, Chesterfield County, and Virginia Beach City Public Schools all operate on similar late-August calendars, though their specific start and end dates — and Spring Break weeks — do not always align. Northern Virginia families often compare Alexandria City Public Schools, Arlington Public Schools, Fairfax County, Loudoun County, and Prince William County because work, childcare, and sibling schedules can cross district lines inside the Washington, D.C. metro area.

Spring Break in Virginia typically falls in late March to mid-April, varying by district. The Washington, D.C. metro area (including Fairfax, Loudoun, Prince William, Arlington, and Alexandria) sees heavy Spring Break travel, particularly to D.C. museums, monuments, and surrounding destinations. Virginia Beach's oceanfront is one of the most popular East Coast Spring Break destinations for Mid-Atlantic and Southern families — hotel rooms book months in advance.

Virginia's military communities include Naval Station Norfolk, Joint Base Langley-Eustis, and several Northern Virginia installations. Virginia Beach City Public Schools serves one of the largest military family communities on the East Coast. Families arriving on PCS orders should contact the destination district early, particularly for late-August school starts.
planningTips: Virginia Beach Spring Break is one of the most popular East Coast destinations for Mid-Atlantic families. Hotel prices surge in late March and early April — book at least 3–4 months in advance.

Northern Virginia districts (Alexandria City, Arlington, Fairfax, Loudoun, and Prince William) do not always share the same Spring Break week. If your family spans multiple districts, verify each separately before booking.

Fairfax County serves a large diplomatic and federal government community — the district is accustomed to mid-year enrollment. Contact the district early if arriving outside the standard enrollment window.

Military families near Naval Station Norfolk or Virginia Beach: contact Virginia Beach City Public Schools upon receipt of PCS orders. The district has extensive experience with military family enrollment transitions.
commonHolidays: Columbus Day / Indigenous Peoples' Day (varies by district)

faqs.q: When do Virginia schools start in 2026?
faqs.a: Most large Virginia districts — including Fairfax County, Prince William County, Loudoun County, Chesterfield County, and Virginia Beach — begin the 2026–2027 school year in late August. Select a district above to see its exact first day of school.

faqs.q: When is Spring Break in Virginia for 2026–2027?
faqs.a: Spring Break in Virginia typically falls in late March to mid-April, varying by district. Northern Virginia districts do not always share the same Spring Break week. Check each district's calendar before booking travel.

faqs.q: What is the Kings Dominion Law in Virginia?
faqs.a: The Kings Dominion Law was a Virginia statute that historically required most public schools to begin no earlier than Labor Day in September, partly to protect summer tourism including the Kings Dominion amusement park near Richmond. The law has since been modified, allowing many districts to start in late August with a local waiver.

faqs.q: How long is Winter Break in Virginia for 2026–2027?
faqs.a: Most Virginia districts observe approximately two weeks of Winter Break from late December into early January. Exact dates vary by district — select a district above to see its specific break window.

faqs.q: When is the last day of school in Virginia for 2026–2027?
faqs.a: Most large Virginia districts end the school year in mid-to-late June. Select a district above to see its exact last day of school for 2026–2027.

faqs.q: Do Fairfax and Loudoun County have the same Spring Break?
faqs.a: Not always. Fairfax County and Loudoun County may schedule Spring Break on the same week but this is not guaranteed. Check both districts' official calendars if your family spans multiple Northern Virginia school systems.

faqs.q: Does Virginia have school on Columbus Day?
faqs.a: It varies by district. Some Virginia districts observe Columbus Day as a school holiday; others observe Indigenous Peoples' Day; and some treat the day as a regular instructional day. Check your specific district's calendar rather than assuming a statewide standard.

faqs.q: Can I add a Virginia school calendar to Google Calendar?
faqs.a: Yes. Each district page on MySchoolDates lets you download an ICS file with school holidays, breaks, and key dates. Import it into Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, or Outlook.
